props.description: When true, overrides the side and align preferences to prevent collisions with boundary edges.

props.name: collisionBoundary
props.description: The element used as the collision boundary. By default this is the viewport, though you can provide additional element(s) to be included in this check.

props.description: The distance in pixels from the boundary edges where collision detection should occur. Accepts a number (same for all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: { top: 20, left: 20 }.

props.description: Whether to hide the content when the trigger becomes fully occluded.

props.description: When true, keyboard navigation will loop from last item to first, and vice versa.

props.name: memoDependencies
props.description: Reactive dependencies that should invalidate the memoized content subtree.

props.name: positionStrategy
props.description: The type of CSS position property to use.

props.name: prioritizePosition
props.description: Force content to be position within the viewport. Might overlap the reference element, which may not be desired.

props.name: reference
props.description: The custom element or virtual element that will be set as the reference to position the floating element. If provided, it will replace the default anchor element.

props.name: side
props.description: The preferred side of the trigger to render against when open. Will be reversed when collisions occur and avoidCollisions is enabled.

props.name: sideFlip
props.description: Flip to the opposite side when colliding with boundary.

props.name: sideOffset
props.description: The distance in pixels from the trigger.

props.name: sticky
props.description: The sticky behavior on the align axis. partial will keep the content in the boundary as long as the trigger is at least partially in the boundary whilst "always" will keep the content in the boundary regardless.

props.name: updatePositionStrategy
props.description: Strategy to update the position of the floating element on every animation frame.

components.name: Tooltip
components.description: Contains all the parts of a tooltip.

props.name: defaultOpen
props.description: The open state of the tooltip when it is initially rendered. Use when you do not need to control its open state.

props.name: delayDuration
props.description: Override the duration given to the Provider to customise the open delay for a specific tooltip.

props.name: disableClosingTrigger
props.description: When true, clicking on trigger will not close the content.

props.name: disabled
props.description: When true, disable tooltip

props.name: disableHoverableContent
props.description: Prevents Tooltip.Content from remaining open when hovering. Disabling this has accessibility consequences. Inherits from Tooltip.Provider.

props.name: ignoreNonKeyboardFocus
props.description: Prevent the tooltip from opening if the focus did not come from the keyboard by matching against the :focus-visible selector. This is useful if you want to avoid opening it when switching browser tabs or closing a dialog.

props.name: open
props.description: The controlled open state of the tooltip.

components.name: TooltipContent
components.description: The component that pops out when the tooltip is open.

props.name: align
props.description: The preferred alignment against the trigger. May change when collisions occur.

props.name: alignOffset
props.description: An offset in pixels from the start or end alignment options.

props.name: ariaLabel
props.description: By default, screenreaders will announce the content inside the component. If this is not descriptive enough, or you have content that cannot be announced, use aria-label as a more descriptive label.

props.name: arrowPadding
props.description: The padding between the arrow and the edges of the content. If your content has border-radius, this will prevent it from overflowing the corners.

props.name: as
props.description: The element or component this component should render as. Can be overwritten by asChild.

props.name: asChild
props.description: Change the default rendered element for the one passed as a child, merging their props and behavior.

props.name: avoidCollisions
props.description: When true, overrides the side and align preferences to prevent collisions with boundary edges.

props.name: collisionBoundary
props.description: The element used as the collision boundary. By default this is the viewport, though you can provide additional element(s) to be included in this check.

props.name: collisionPadding
props.description: The distance in pixels from the boundary edges where collision detection should occur. Accepts a number (same for all sides), or a partial padding object, for example: { top: 20, left: 20 }.

props.name: forceMount
props.description: Used to force mounting when more control is needed. Useful when controlling animation with Vue animation libraries.

props.name: hideWhenDetached
props.description: Whether to hide the content when the trigger becomes fully occluded.

props.name: positionStrategy
props.description: The type of CSS position property to use.

props.name: side
props.description: The preferred side of the trigger to render against when open. Will be reversed when collisions occur and avoidCollisions is enabled.

props.name: sideOffset
props.description: The distance in pixels from the trigger.

props.name: sticky
props.description: The sticky behavior on the align axis. partial will keep the content in the boundary as long as the trigger is at least partially in the boundary whilst "always" will keep the content in the boundary regardless.

props.name: updatePositionStrategy
props.description: Strategy to update the position of the floating element on every animation frame.

components.name: TooltipProvider
components.description: Wraps your app to provide global functionality to your tooltips.

props.name: content
props.description: Default settings that will be used by all tooltip components.

props.name: delayDuration
props.description: The duration from when the pointer enters the trigger until the tooltip gets opened.

props.name: disableClosingTrigger
props.description: When true, clicking on trigger will not close the content.

props.name: disabled
props.description: When true, disable tooltip

props.name: disableHoverableContent
props.description: When true, trying to hover the content will result in the tooltip closing as the pointer leaves the trigger.

props.name: ignoreNonKeyboardFocus
props.description: Prevent the tooltip from opening if the focus did not come from the keyboard by matching against the :focus-visible selector. This is useful if you want to avoid opening it when switching browser tabs or closing a dialog.

props.name: skipDelayDuration
props.description: How much time a user has to enter another trigger without incurring a delay again.

components.name: TooltipTrigger
components.description: The button that toggles the tooltip. By default, the TooltipContent will position itself against the trigger.

props.name: as
props.description: The element or component this component should render as. Can be overwritten by asChild.

props.name: asChild
props.description: Change the default rendered element for the one passed as a child, merging their props and behavior.

props.name: reference
props.description: The reference (or anchor) element that is being referred to for positioning. If not provided will use the current component as anchor.
